name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: [Sign the card] Congratulate these ③② women on their victories
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cb9/24c69fd9/16d19f68d/189e9964/331443649/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact&source=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact")
---

Peter,

I could NOT be more proud of our candidates and this community today.

We endorsed women in more than a dozen states yesterday. So far, pending recounts and runoffs, at least **32 have WON their elections.**

That is not a trickle. It is a wave. And it is a sign the resistance is real; it is here to stay; and it is getting elected to office.

**[Sign our card congratulating the 32 EMILY's List women who won their elections yesterday.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cb9/24c69fd9/16d19f68d/189e9965/331443649/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171108_email_Congrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Sign+our+card+congratulating+the+32+EMIL&utm_campaign=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact&source=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact")**
[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cb9/24c69fd9/16d19f68d/189e9966/331443649/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171108_email_Congrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact&source=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact")
Running for office is an enormous act of courage.

As a former candidate, I know firsthand that it is scary to put yourself out there, to expose your family to personal attacks, to put everything into working for something you're passionate about.

I am so glad these outstanding women made the choice to run, and now they are going to make their communities and this country a better place.

**Send your congratulations to the 32 women you helped elect yesterday:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Send-Congrats](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cb9/24c69fd9/16d19f68d/189e9967/331443649/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171108_email_Congrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSendCongrats&utm_campaign=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact&source=WQENEK21N_digital_email_20171108_Congrats_inact")**

Thank you -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

name: Jay Inslee
subject: We just took back the legislature in Washington state.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Last night's victories are a sign that voters want progress.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTIzMTg5MzgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDc3Mzc4MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Great news for Washington state: With Manka Dhingra's decisive win in the 45th Legislative District, Democrats have taken back the legislature. From Washington state to Virginia, we are sending a clear message to Donald Trump on the first anniversary of his election: The American people are done with his divisive agenda. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzMTg5MzgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDc3Mzc4MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) [**Last night 's decisive wins are a clear indication that voters are ready for progress. Chip in now to make sure we see that progress through in 2018 and beyond.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzMTg5MzgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDc3Mzc4MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) For the first time since I've been governor, Democrats have control of both chambers of the legislature. We've had solid bipartisan successes in Olympia over the past few years, and now I look forward to action on issues that have stalled for too long. This victory is an opportunity -- no, a **mandate** \-- to make progress for Washington on climate change, gun regulations, and voting rights -- to move us forward on the issues that matter to people's lives. Americans are hungry for a change. But we can't let these victories placate us -- we need to seize this moment. No seat was won without massive effort from volunteers like you, lacing up your boots to spread the message and mobilize your neighbors. We have just proven that these efforts can work to beat Trump and the GOP. Now, we have to prepare to take back power at a national level in 2018. Let's let Washington lead as an example for the nation. **Make sure we have the power we need to push forward our progressive agenda and prepare for 2018 by donating right now.** | [CONTRIBUTE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIzMTg5MzgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDc3Mzc4MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

name: The White House
subject: Advancing American and Global Prosperity
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed96758ec0e5384b9d9e0a04530bb2291af8cf45756b71f40c41652c5e641c7448c4efef5e520f8af2d9e6db0628b8e045d

Saying “No” to a Broken Tax Code

President Donald J. Trump stated, "We believe every-day Americans know better how to spend their own money than the federal bureaucracy, and we want to help them keep as much of that hard-earned money as we can." Unfortunately, the current tax code hurts American job-creators, families, and businesses. Under President Trump's tax plan, Americans will be treated fairly, keep more of their hard-earned money, and companies will be incentivized to bring jobs back to the United States.

Read what the President and others have said about tax reform
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed9e690a1a19b6c023a428743d41aa75fdeec2a372047d3cf6c6958681de124938b825d601bff12e2a8fdedf8acfed8b857
here.

President Trump in China

On Wednesday, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump traveled to Beijing, People’s Republic of China, where they were welcomed by President Xi Jingping and Madame Peng Liyuan for tea, a tour of the Forbidden City, and a viewing of an opera performance in the evening. President Trump and President Xi discussed ways that the United States and China can continue to apply pressure on North Korea and strengthen the international resolve to de-nuclearize the regime entirely. Furthermore, President Trump stressed to President Xi that China’s unfair trade practices and America’s massive trade deficit with China is not sustainable and highlighted the Trump Administration’s determination to defend America’s economic interests.

Read President Trump and President Xi’s joint press statement
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed988363941019fdf4cf811dbd9d1da2269ff414b9cfe7871390a619c7538b312ecde53fbea4f0980de8c11df0f61ae9e11
here.

Vice President, Second Lady Visit Sutherland Springs

Yesterday afternoon, Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence departed Washington, D.C. on Air Force Two en route to San Antonio, Texas. Together, they visited victims injured in the tragic First Baptist Church shooting, followed by a briefing from local law enforcement officials. The Vice President and First Lady spent the evening meeting with families of victims and offering remarks at a prayer vigil which took place at a local high school.

"My fellow Americans, we live in challenging times. It seems like too often we hear of another tragedy, another senseless act of violence against the innocent. In these times I expect it’s easy for some to lose heart. But as the good people of Sutherland Springs taught the nation this week, faith is the antidote to fear and despair. Faith is now and always has been our source of strength and the summit of our national life," the Vice President said.

Read the full remarks
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed962f8f70e0e8920c0522528918abf3876584e0181cd241d5484c50a9c9bfe3ff53ba2570783364eb79deb6b6a6a858cc0
here .

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and President Xi of China | November 8, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Tonight, President Trump will depart Beijing, People’s Republic of China, en route to Da Nang, Vietnam. After arriving in Da Nang, the President will deliver remarks to the APEC CEO Summit, participate in a veterans meet and greet, and participate in an official welcome for APEC leaders and spouses. Later, President Trump will attend a gala dinner and cultural performance.

Vice President Mike Pence, joined by Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta, will travel to Plainfield, Indiana. There, the Vice President, Secretary Acosta, and Governor Eric Holcomb (R-IN) will meet with local business leaders and Hoosier families to discuss the need for tax reform in the United States.

COMING UP

President Trump continues to travel in Asia this week. After concluding his trip in China, the President will visit Vietnam and the Philippines.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed9a71fa9b770a384b003f442af47593049c141b5a3733f9f92d861027d405f7df624f7f7045ad242c554d7ecbcf342d6f1
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d0868abdb0639ed93906360b487949073b3528fde6da65dea6eeafe905eb6d392afcc52b69bd1146a5b36631581436cf5f53fa343ca45dfd
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d490431d3afebd8c970e31864c67fcd6b875a29097d67625d5bb6632333fabc6429e410cb4962e0307e60e033a2f21fb13
Unsubscribe

name: Democrats.org
subject: Fwd: This is just the beginning
body:
Democrats won BIG on Tuesday!
---
Tuesday night was a historic win for us, Derek. We walked away with victories in Virginia, New Jersey, and beyond.

Those wins weren't a fluke or just a reaction to Donald Trump. They're what happens when we lead with a strong ground game, harness the latest tools and technology, and invest in Democrats at ALL levels -- from the school board to the Oval Office.

So if you think Tuesday's wins were something, take a minute and think of what we can accomplish in Alabama's Senate special election and in 2018.

**Then chip in $10 or more to keep winning and elect more Democrats:

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405239ea/4067b0a/40966835a/7e592dbf/2641035937/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Elect-Democrats-2017-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171109_joc_nd&source=em_20171109_joc_nd&refcode=em_20171109_joc_nd")
** Thanks,

Jess

Jess O'Connell
Chief Executive Officer
Democratic National Committee

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
Sender: Tom Perez
Date: Tue, Nov 7, 2017
Subject: This is just the beginning

Because of you, Democrats just won big tonight in Virginia and New Jersey! What we're doing is working.

This election cycle, the Democratic National Committee invested more in boots on the ground and grassroots and digital organizing than in any "off-year" before. Good old-fashioned organizing paired with the latest technology and tools helped put our candidates over the top.

I am so proud of the campaigns run by Virginia's Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring, New Jersey's Phil Murphy and Sheila Oliver, and Democrats all over the country, up and down the ballot. These candidates worked hard day in and day out fighting to represent their states, and I know that they will take that same spirit and fight into their terms.

But here's the thing: All of this is just the beginning.

We are going to keep investing in state parties and supporting Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office. And if we continue to channel our energy into powering this movement, there's no doubt in my mind that we will see wins like this in 2018, 2020, and beyond.

**If you're even half as fired up as I am, chip in $10 or more right now to keep electing Democrats.**

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

I am so proud of the campaigns run by Virginia's Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring, New Jersey's Phil Murphy and Sheila Oliver, and Democrats all over the country, up and down the ballot. These candidates worked hard day in and day out fighting to represent their states, and I know that they will take that same spirit and fight into their terms. But here's the thing: All of this is just the beginning. We are going to keep investing in state parties and supporting Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office. And if we continue to channel our energy into powering this movement, there's no doubt in my mind that we will see wins like this in 2018, 2020, and beyond. If you're even half as fired up as I am, chip in $10 or more right now to keep electing Democrats. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. "" "" "" "" "" "" Thanks. Keep up the hard work and let's keep winning. These candidates won because of people like you volunteering, making calls, knocking doors, and yes, donating. And last night is proof that what we're doing -- what YOU are doing -- is working.

name: Jill Vogel
subject: Thank You
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Virginia45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Dear Peter,
In all of the hoopla of Election Night and wind down yesterday with family and friends, I did not get to write my final campaign email to say thank you to everyone who worked so hard for us in election 2017.
First, even though we did not win this has still been a very special time to celebrate a lot of hard work and give thanks for the many people who gave so much to help and support us. We did the absolute best that we could and all I can ever say to the people who supported us and our volunteers who gave thousands of hours of work is THANK YOU!!!!
This campaign has meant so much to me and so have the countless friendships that I have made along the trail. I always said that it isn't just about winning, that I wanted our campaign to mean something and it did. We highlighted issues that never get attention. We visited communities where candidates never go. We had an insane work ethic. We ran a campaign that was positive and inclusive and we got more votes than any other Republican candidate for statewide office in Virginia history!!
While I am disappointed, I was prepared for whatever happened. I knew I could end our campaign having nothing left to give and with no regrets.
I want to congratulate Justin Fairfax and his family on their victory. I know how excited they must be and that he has some hectic, but fun, training ahead with the Clerk of the Senate to get ready for the Senate session.
I want to thank Alex, my wonderful husband who sacrificed the most in this campaign, and my children who were so proud of me and so supportive. I want to thank my dad who made me the person I am. He is always optimistic, thoughtful, and will help anyone, anytime and taught me that that is what matters in life. He and Ann deserve special thanks. My mom died during this campaign and I cannot imagine what she would think right this minute. She would be proud and just obsess about whether I was eating and sleeping enough.
A very special thank you to my ticket mates, Ed and John, who were incredible to work with and who have my deepest respect and admiration for so much hard work not just in these campaigns, but for Virginia.
Finally, thank you to our campaign team who will have my loyalty forever. You could not find finer or more hard working people anywhere. I mean that and people who know some of them can attest that we have some incredible people in Chris, Rollin, Angie, Jenna, Eloise, Carli, Tracy, Devon, Jack, Lynn, and Thomas. That team truly left nothing on the field in this race. These are not campaign staff people to me, they are family and always will be.
I also want to emphasize one last thing. I am not spending one minute wallowing in any sadness over losing an election. I have had so much fun spending time with family and friends and having things slow down. I have a lot more to be grateful for than I ever do to be sad about and I want my supporters to know that we all do. We can still work for all of the things we care about and always make it mean something. Thank you all!!
Sincerely,
---
|
---
| Jill Vogel

I wanted to take a moment and personally reach out about our big win with Rick Kriseman in St. Petersberg, as well as the wins in Virginia, Washington State and New Jersey.

These wins proved that Democrats aren't in disarray, but working harder than ever to rebuild our party after 2016.

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: We won't back down to the Bully-in-Chief
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| I can hardly believe it. Yesterday marked a year since Donald Trump was elected president. So many absurd things have happened since then, it's almost impossible to keep track. We need to elect more Democrats to office so we can get the country back on track. [_**Let's make sure we have the grassroots strength to organize, mobilize, and win in 2018 and beyond. Give $5, $10, or even more, if you can.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e6a/622b7b5b/721e56b/307bac1c/1791164933/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171108?refcode=dwse171108_m&recurring=true&amount=5") "Alternative facts." "Fake news." Repeated attempts to rip health care from millions of Americans. Destroying all progress against climate change. I won't even attempt to count the number of mind-boggling Twitter rants that have come from the President, often in the dead of night. I think it goes without saying that this is what we were worried about that night, one year ago -- instability, dishonesty, and turmoil. But it's in times that we truly find our strength. Hundreds of thousands of us have taken to the streets in support of women's rights, equality, and science. We've repelled every Republican attempt to destroy access to health care. **We haven't backed down to the Bully-in-Chief or his allies in Congress -- we've grown stronger, together.** And as long as we keep that up, I'm confident things will look and feel quite different on Election Night 2018. [_**I need you with me, every step of the way. To keep this network going strong, we need pick up the pace. So please, if you can, stand with me by giving $5, $10, or more.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e6a/622b7b5b/721e56b/307bac1c/1791164933/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171108?refcode=dwse171108_m&recurring=true&amount=5") I fully intend to continue fighting back against Trump and his backward agenda. I hope I can count on you to continue fighting beside me. Thank you. Debbie | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e6a/622b7b5b/721e56b/307bac1c/1791164933/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171108?refcode=dwse171108_m&recurring=true&amount=5")

subject: Important message from Tim Kaine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Kaine for Virginia

Derek: We wanted to make sure you saw this important message from Tim last night. Critical House of Delegates races in Virginia are going into recounts, and we have a shot at a Democratic majority.

This is a really big deal -- please read Tim's note, then pitch in whatever you can afford to help fund these recounts.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democratic-delegates?refcode=20171109&amount=25 ]

Thank you so much.

-- Team Kaine

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Tim Kaine
Subject: Recounts

Friend --

I'm so proud of Virginia. When I first ran for statewide office in 2001, all statewide officeholders were Republican, and we were deep red in presidential politics. Today, Democrats have won 10 statewide races in a row. All of our statewide elected officials are Democrats. We've gone blue for three presidential races in a row. Four of the last five Governors have been Democrats. And we've had huge wins in the House of Delegates.

*But this is really important: We are so close to flipping the House of Delegates into Democratic control -- and six races from last night's election are likely to go into recounts.*

That means if just three of those six are officially called for Democrats, we will gain a majority and be able to take decisive action on issues like Medicaid expansion and redistricting reform. But Democrats need your help to fund these recounts. Will you chip in right now to make sure we have the resources we need?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democratic-delegates?refcode=20171109&amount=25 ]

This is just incredible. Virginia is the biggest turnaround in recent American politics, and a Democratic majority in the House of Delegates would push us into solidly blue territory.

The secret to our success? Good jobs, education, health care for all. We fight for everyday people instead of fighting each other, and we wholeheartedly reject divisive Trump-style politics.

Please pitch in right now to help fund these critical recounts.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democratic-delegates?refcode=20171109&amount=25 ]

Thank you so much.

Tim Kaine

DONATE

Paid for by Kaine for Virginia

name: LegitPAC Team
subject: Virginia shows how to win Alabama
body:
[ Legit ]LegitPAC

Friend,

Democrat Ralph Northam outperformed pre-election polls by about 6
points en route to beating Republican Ed Gillespie in Virginia’s
gubernatorial election Tuesday night. And democratic legitimacy was
most definitely on the ballot.

Gillespie ran with an explicit campaign of disenfranchisement and
other attacks on legitimacy. But Northam won running on policies like
restoring the ability to vote to former felons. That’s why LegitPAC
endorsed Northam.

Now there’s another big race coming up, where disgraced Republican
judge Roy Moore is running to tear down legitimacy in his race against
Democratic attorney Doug Jones for Senate in Alabama.

Jones has a tough route to victory, but the stakes for legitimacy are
just as high as they were in Virginia -- and some polls have shown the
Alabama Senate race within a few points. This race is winnable if we
act now.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/1988?t=1002&akid=390%2E356062%2EWfNfjj ]Contribute $3 to Doug Jones’ campaign for
Alabama Senate today. Help build on Virginia’s victories for
legitimacy.

Turnout in Virginia this week was the highest in two decades.
Democratic legitimacy was on the ballot, and Virginia voters were
motivated. If we help make sure Doug Jones has the resources to
motivate Alabama voters, this race is winnable.

Thank you for standing up for legitimacy today,

LegitPAC Team

subject: What we saw
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
What we saw on Tuesday was nothing short of amazing. |
---

| | Peter,

What we saw on Tuesday was nothing short of amazing. Democrats pulled out wins in Virginia, Georgia, Washington State, North Carolina, New Jersey, and New York in tough, competitive races just like Joe's and the reason is simple: **Folks showed up and did the hard work.**

They got up early to knock doors every day. They phone banked every night. They rallied in all sorts of weather. And their efforts paid off.

**It 's going to take the same hard work to send Joe back to the Senate, but we're ready. Yesterday Peter emailed you about how BIG Tuesday's results were, and so many of you responded that we wanted to ask again: _Will you chip in to become a Founding Donor of our 2018 campaign and help us keep fighting? body:
| __
Earlier today, The Washington Post reported on serious and deeply-troubling conduct by Roy Moore, the Republican Senate candidate in Alabama.

The report regarding a sexual assault by Moore, then 32-years old, against a 14-year old Alabama teenager included interviews with several women in the community who reluctantly came forward to answer inquiries by the Post.

Three other women have also stepped forward with their own statements of improper and unwanted sexual conduct against them by Moore.

There can be no equivocation or stalling. Roy Moore does not belong in the Senate.
